title: Lesson 343
transcript: Okay, we just talked about different types of testing. And one of the types of testing we talked about, a form of dynamic testing is fuzzing. We just have to dig into fuzzing in a little more detail. There's actually two major types of fuzzing, mutational fuzzers and generation or generation fuzzers. And you can see it here in the blue text, the kind of major difference between them. Mutation fuzzers are kind of considered to be dumb fuzzers and generational fuzzers are considered to be intelligent fuzzers. What does this mean? Because what they're both doing is generating essentially very random, very chaotic input, right? Because remember, that's the whole point of a fuzzer is to provide very chaotic input to a system, right? Because programmers tend to be very logical people. They expect logical input, which will provide logical output. If you throw chaos at a system, it'll often cause that system to fail in unique and interesting ways. And so a mutation or dumb fuzzer doesn't really have any understanding of the application and how the application works. It's truly just providing completely random input. And that's why it's considered a dumb fuzzer because it doesn't have any understanding of the application that it's fuzzing. Whereas generation fuzzers, they do have at least a basic understanding of the application. They understand, for instance, are they fuzzing a username or a password or an email address as simple examples. They have a little bit of understanding of what input is being expected by the application for various input fields and input parameters. And the fuzzer will modify its approach based on these different inputs, right? Like the way that you would fuzz a file upload versus a password is a little bit different. And so that's the idea behind a generation fuzzer, an intelligent fuzzer. So just remember that basic definition of the exam. A mutation fuzzer is essentially a dumb fuzzer. And a generation fuzzer is an intelligent fuzzer because it understands the application in a little more detail that it's actually fuzzing.
